# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: External PR Build and Test
on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read # Needed for actions/checkout
  pull-requests: write # Needed for actions/github-script to post comments

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR head branch
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          # Checkout the head commit of the pull request, not the merge ref.
          # This ensures we test the actual code submitted by the contributor.
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}

      - name: Setup Node.js # Example: adapt for your project's language/runtime
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18' # Specify your project's Node.js version

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci # Use 'npm ci' for clean installs in CI environments

      - name: Run tests
        id: run_tests
        # Redirect stdout and stderr to a file, and print to console using tee.
        # Use `|| true` to prevent the step from failing immediately, allowing us to capture output.
        run: |
          npm test 2>&1 | tee test_output.txt
          echo "TEST_EXIT_CODE=$?" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT" # Capture exit code of npm test
        continue-on-error: true # Allow subsequent steps to run even if tests fail

      - name: Read test output and determine status
        id: process_output
        run: |
          TEST_OUTPUT=$(<test_output.txt)
          # Store the full test output as a multi-line step output
          echo "full_output<<EOF" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "${TEST_OUTPUT}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "EOF" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          # Determine the status message based on the captured exit code
          if [ "${{ steps.run_tests.outputs.TEST_EXIT_CODE }}" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "status=✅ Tests passed!" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "status=❌ Tests failed!" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Post test results as a PR comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v6
        if: always() # Ensure this step always runs to post results
        with:
          script: |
            const { owner, repo, issue_number } = context.issue;
            const testOutput = `${{ steps.process_output.outputs.full_output }}`;
            const testStatus = `${{ steps.process_output.outputs.status }}`;

            const commentBody = `
            ### External PR Test Results

            ${testStatus}

            <details>
            <summary>Click to view full test output</summary>

            \`\`\`
            ${testOutput}
            \`\`\`

            </details>

            _Ran on commit \`${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\`_
            `;

            await github.rest.issues.createComment({
              owner,
              repo,
              issue_number,
              body: commentBody
            });

      - name: Fail job if tests actually failed
        if: ${{ steps.run_tests.outputs.TEST_EXIT_CODE != 0 }}
        run: |
          echo "Tests failed. Marking the overall job as failed."
          exit 1 # This will cause the entire job to fail if tests failed
